topics: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) None Course Type Lab Course Objectives: 1. To impart practical knowledge on various industrial automation technologies. 2. To understand the functioning and programming of Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs). 3. To develop skills in electro-pneumatic and electro-hydraulic systems. 4. To simulate and control industrial processes using SCADA. 5. To familiarize with Automation Studio software for designing and simulating automation systems Expt. No. Experiments 1 Introduction to PLC o Understanding PLC hardware and software. o Basic programming in Ladder Logic. 2 PLC Programming - I o Development of basic automation tasks using PLC. o Simple start-stop circuits. 3 PLC Programming - II o Advanced PLC programming concepts. o Timer and counter functions. 4 Basic Pneumatic Systems o Introduction to pneumatic components. o Designing and implementing basic pneumatic circuits. 5 Electro-Pneumatic Systems o Integration of electrical control with pneumatic systems. o Troubleshooting electro-pneumatic circuits. 6 Basic Hydraulic Systems o Introduction to hydraulic components and systems. o Designing basic hydraulic circuits. 7 Electro-Hydraulic Systems o Integrating electrical control with hydraulic systems. o Practical implementation and troubleshooting. 8 Introduction to SCADA Systems o Basics of SCADA. o Configuring SCADA for monitoring and control applications. 9 SCADA Simulation o Simulating industrial processes using SCADA. o Developing human-machine interfaces (HMIs). 10 Automation Studio Software o Designing and simulating automation systems using Automation Studio. o Final project integrating PLC
pneumatic
hydraulic
and SCADA systems. 11 Comprehensive Project 1  Experiment: Combine PLC and SCADA systems into a single automated process. 12 Comprehensive Project 2  Experiment: Combine PLC
electro-pneumatic
and electro-hydraulic systems into a single automated process. Course Assessment Method (CIE: 50 marks
ESE: 50 marks) Continuous Internal Evaluation Marks (CIE): Attendance Preparation/Pre-Lab Work experiments

Viva and Timely completion of Lab Reports / Record (Continuous Assessment) Internal Examination Total 5 25 20 50 End Semester Examination Marks (ESE): Procedure/ Preparatory work/Design/ Algorithm Conduct of experiment/ Execution of work/ troubleshooting/ Programming Result with valid inference/ Quality of Output Viva voce Record Total 10 15 10 10 5 50  Submission of Record: Students shall be allowed for the end semester examination only upon submitting the duly certified record.  Endorsement by External Examiner: The external examiner shall endorse the record Course Outcomes (COs) At the end of the course students should be able to: Course Outcome Bloom’s Knowledge Level (KL) CO1 Demonstrate the ability to program and troubleshoot PLCs. K3 CO2 Apply knowledge to design and implement electro-pneumatic systems. K3 CO3 Apply knowledge to design and implement electro-hydraulic systems. K3 CO4 Develop and simulate SCADA-based projects. K4 CO5 Utilize Automation Studio software for creating automation solutions. K4 Note: K1- Remember

9 https://onlinecourses.swayam2.ac.in/nou24_ee02/preview Continuous Assessment (25 Marks) 1. Preparation and Pre-Lab Work (7 Marks)  Pre-Lab Assignments: Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment.  Understanding of Theory: Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. 2. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks)  Procedure and Execution: Adherence to correct procedures
accurate execution of experiments
and following safety protocols.  Skill Proficiency: Proficiency in handling equipment
accuracy in observations
and troubleshooting skills during the experiments.  Teamwork: Collaboration and participation in group experiments. 3. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks)  Quality of Reports: Clarity
completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments

data analysis and conclusions.  Timely Submission: Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. 4. Viva Voce (5 Marks)  Oral Examination: Ability to explain the experiment
results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging: The final marks for preparation
conduct of experiments
viva

and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) 1. Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks)  Procedure Understanding and Description: Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved.  Preliminary Work and Planning: Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment.  Algorithm Development: Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment.  Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks)  Setup and Execution: Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks)  Accuracy of Results: Precision and correctness of the obtained results.  Analysis and Interpretation: Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks)  Ability to explain the experiment
procedure results and answer related questions  Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. 5. Record (5 Marks)  Completeness
clarity
and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER S5 ROBOT OPERATING SYSTEM (ROS) LAB
